doc_title: OSHA Technical Manual - Section IV, Chapter 6, Combustible Dusts

steps.text: Read and become familiar with the NEP (National Emphasis Program).
Recognize Combustible Dust Characteristics and Combustible Dust Hazards.
Recognize the Common Signs that a Combustible Dust Hazard is Present.
Determine how to safely conduct the inspection.
Perform inspections of the dust collectors and systems.
Collect representative samples of different types of combustible dusts at the facility.
Determine the tests to request from the SLTC Laboratory.
Safely package and ship the samples to the SLTC.
Thoroughly document inspection observations and findings by photos, videos, sketches, notes, drawings, company documents, and/or other means.
Contact supervisors and/or others with experience to seek help and answers to questions.
Identify the chemical composition of the dust to determine if it could be combustible.

preconditions.text: Before referring to this OTM chapter.
Whenever help or answers to questions are needed.

constraints.text: Combustible dust inspections can be complicated.
Over half of all combustible dust explosions occur within dust collectors.
Sampling must follow safe sampling procedures.
Chemicals and compounds react with oxygen at varying rates and burn with varying energies.
